value: ASIC-resistant Autolykos v2 algorithm ensuring fair mining and network security
Autolykos Mining Algorithm | ASIC-Resistant GPU Mining on Ergo
Mine Ergo with GPUs using Autolykos v2 - the energy-efficient, ASIC-resistant algorithm. Start solo mining ERG profitably with consumer hardware. Complete mining guide included.
autolykos, ergo mining, ASIC resistant, GPU mining, proof of work, secure mining algorithm
Memory-hard algorithm prevents ASIC dominance and keeps mining decentralized
More efficient than Bitcoin's SHA-256 while maintaining security guarantees
GPU-friendly design ensures anyone can participate in network security
Prevents mining centralization through ASIC resistance
Reduces energy consumption compared to traditional PoW
Enables profitable mining with consumer hardware
Maintains network security through memory-hard puzzles
Supports fair distribution of mining rewards
Autolykos v2 is Ergo's memory-hard Proof-of-Work algorithm designed to be ASIC-resistant and GPU-friendly, keeping mining decentralized.
Autolykos requires significant memory bandwidth making ASIC development economically unviable while remaining efficient on consumer GPUs.
Autolykos is more energy-efficient than Bitcoin's SHA-256, requiring less electricity per hash while maintaining security.
Can I mine Ergo with my GPU?
Yes! Autolykos is designed for GPU mining. Most modern GPUs can mine ERG profitably, making it accessible to home miners.
The memory-hard nature of Autolykos makes it expensive to develop specialized ASICs, keeping mining accessible to individual miners with consumer hardware.
What hardware do I need to mine Ergo?
You need a modern GPU with at least 4GB VRAM. Popular choices include NVIDIA RTX series and AMD RX series graphics cards.
The foundation of Ergo's advanced smart contract capabilities
Extended UTXO combines Bitcoin's proven security model with powerful smart contract functionality via ErgoScript, enabling parallel execution and eliminating entire classes of vulnerabilities.
eUTXO Model: Parallel Smart Contracts | Ergo
Learn how Ergo's Extended UTXO model enables parallel execution, MEV resistance, and composable DeFi. No re-entrancy attacks by design.
eUTXO, Extended UTXO, UTXO model, parallel execution, smart contracts, MEV resistance, composability, Ergo blockchain, DeFi security, box model
Each UTXO is a 'box' containing value, data, and a guarding script that defines spending conditions.
Boxes are immutable — once created, they cannot be modified, only consumed and new ones created.
The UTXO model eliminates re-entrancy attacks by design — no mutable state to exploit.
Transactions touching different boxes can execute in parallel, improving scalability.
Smart contracts only need to verify their own inputs, not global blockchain state.
Complex DeFi protocols built from simple, reusable box patterns.
No global balances — only unspent outputs
Global state with account balances and storage
Natural transaction privacy through separate boxes
Modular outputs compose into complex protocols
Each order is a separate box, enabling parallel matching and atomic swaps
Collateral locked in boxes with clear liquidation conditions
Reserve boxes track collateral independently for each position
Bid boxes compete without blocking each other

The eUTXO model simplifies smart contract development by treating everything as boxes with clear spending conditions. No complex state management, no re-entrancy concerns — just straightforward logic.
How is eUTXO different from Bitcoin's basic UTXO?

While Bitcoin's UTXO model only tracks value transfers, Ergo's eUTXO extends this with arbitrary data storage in boxes and Turing-complete smart contracts via ErgoScript. This enables complex DeFi applications while keeping UTXO benefits.
Can eUTXO handle complex DeFi applications?
Yes! Ergo has DEXes (Spectrum), lending protocols, stablecoins (SigmaUSD), and more. The eUTXO model actually makes these more secure by preventing re-entrancy and enabling parallel execution.
Why is the eUTXO model more scalable?
Independent boxes enable parallel transaction processing. Unlike account models where state contention forces sequential execution, eUTXO transactions touching different boxes can run simultaneously.
How do smart contracts work in eUTXO?

Each box has a guard script that defines its spending conditions. When spending a box, the script is executed with provided inputs. If it returns true, the box can be spent. This is simpler and more predictable than account-based execution.

Gradually introduce new privacy features while maintaining backward compatibility

Common questions about velvet forks and their implementation
What exactly is a velvet fork?

A velvet fork is Ergo's approach to protocol upgrades that maintains complete backward compatibility while enabling new features. Unlike hard or soft forks, velvet forks never break existing functionality.
How do velvet forks differ from soft forks?
While soft forks tighten existing rules, velvet forks can add entirely new features without affecting nodes that don't upgrade. This allows for more flexible protocol evolution.
Yes, velvet forks maintain the same security guarantees as the base protocol. New features are designed to be optional and don't compromise the security of existing functionality.
Do I need to upgrade my node for velvet forks?
No, upgrades are optional. Your node will continue to work normally, though you won't have access to new features until you choose to upgrade.
Ergo's storage rent system ensures long-term network health by preventing blockchain bloat and creating a predictable cost for data storage.
Storage Rent Explained | Sustainable Blockchain Economics on Ergo
Revolutionary economic model solving blockchain bloat forever. Learn how Storage Rent ensures sustainable blockchain size, perpetual miner rewards, and efficient resource usage.
Storage Rent | The Solution to Blockchain Sustainability
How Ergo prevents blockchain bloat while ensuring perpetual network security through Storage Rent.
Storage Rent | Solving Blockchain Bloat Forever
Sustainable economics: automatic cleanup, perpetual miner rewards, efficient blockchain.
Traditional blockchains grow indefinitely, making full node operation increasingly expensive
Millions in lost or forgotten coins create permanent blockchain overhead
Block rewards decrease over time, threatening long-term security
Small fee charged for continued storage
If rent isn't paid, remaining value goes to miners
How Storage Rent solves blockchain bloat and ensures long-term sustainability
Storage Rent is a mechanism where unused coins pay a minimal fee after 4 years of inactivity, preventing blockchain bloat and providing permanent miner rewards beyond emission.
How much does Storage Rent cost?
Storage Rent costs approximately 0.14 ERG per UTXO every 4 years. Active users never pay rent as any transaction resets the timer.
Storage Rent ensures blockchain sustainability by preventing unlimited growth, keeping the network lightweight, and providing perpetual security through miner rewards after emission ends.
Create and trade tokens and NFTs directly at the protocol level
No smart contracts needed - just simple, secure, and cost-effective native assets. Issue tokens, stablecoins, or NFTs in a single transaction with protocol-level security guarantees.
No smart contracts needed - just simple, secure, and cost-effective native assets. Issue tokens, stablecoins, or NFTs in a single transaction with protocol-level security guarantees.
Unlike other blockchains that require complex smart contracts, Ergo treats custom tokens as first-class citizens
Unlike other blockchains that require complex smart contracts, Ergo treats custom tokens as first-class citizens
Native tokens are first-class citizens, as secure as ERG itself
Create tokens without complex code or contracts
Issue tokens in a single transaction
Minimal fees for token creation and transfers
Create any type of digital asset with native protocol support
Applications that benefit from native token creation
Create custom currencies and utility tokens
Issue stable assets with protocol security
Create tokens with just a few lines of code

// Token creation parameters
val tokenName = "MyToken"
val tokenDescription = "My awesome token on Ergo"
val tokenDecimals = 2
val tokenAmount = 1000000L

// Create token in a single transaction
val token = Minting.issueToken(
  ergoClient = client,
  tokenName = tokenName,
  tokenDescription = tokenDescription,
  tokenAmount = tokenAmount,
  tokenDecimals = tokenDecimals,
  mintingAddress = address,
  txFee = Parameters.MinFee
)

// Token is now live on the blockchain!
println(s"Token ID: ${token.getId}")
println(s"Token created in TX: ${token.creationTx}")
No smart contract deployment, no complex setup - just simple, native token creation.
A thriving ecosystem of tools and platforms for native tokens
What are native tokens on Ergo?
Native tokens are first-class assets on Ergo that can be created and traded directly at the protocol level without smart contracts. They have the same security guarantees as ERG itself.
How much does it cost to create a token?
Creating a token on Ergo costs approximately $0.01 in transaction fees. This is significantly cheaper than other blockchains that require smart contract deployment.
Can I create NFTs on Ergo?
Yes, NFTs are created as native tokens with a supply of 1. They support rich metadata including images, descriptions, and custom properties stored on IPFS.
Are native tokens compatible with DeFi?
Yes, native tokens have full DeFi compatibility and can be used in all Ergo applications and protocols without special integration requirements.
